notes: This was about a call with Efraim
rich_text:
I didnt get around to listen yet, sorry


I just want you too know this though.


After talking to you, I haven't had an urge or anything yet, despite the fact the stress is building up.


Its possible I "got it out of my system" since the last time I messed up, and its just recharging.


But I think this.


This is the first time I actually spoke to someone about this, that actually understands. Ive spoken to my mashgiach and rosh yeshiva about this in the past, and although they were sympathetic, they didn't understand.


The fact I spoke to you, and you told me about yourself and others, just knowing first hand I'm not alone, both in the struggle and that there is help, I feel takes the pressure off.


When I feel that this is my problem alone to deal with, I give up fast when the struggle comes.


Anyway I do intend to listen to everything, and take advantage of all the help that's being offered, but I just want to let you know about how I feel now

notes: Received via HHM, "I thought you would like seeing this from a relatively new "chaver" of mine..."
rich_text:
Good morning,

I woke up this morning to the completely redesigned GYE platform, and I felt I needed to take a moment to thank you.

I’m 24 years old, married and a father of kids, and have been struggling for the past 11 years. Over that time, I tried many different forms of help, including therapy and other methods. GYE was the only platform that gave me a long, holy, clean streak. I did go through a few difficult weeks recently, but BH, thanks to GYE and the many friends I’ve made here, I’m already back on track and currently on my 8th day.

The redesigned platform genuinely excites me, and I can clearly feel that it will strongly help me push toward a final clean life iy”h.

The dashboard — wow. Seeing progress counted down to the second makes every moment feel like a win. Every second is a new success, and that perspective is incredibly encouraging. I can honestly just sit and watch the timer and think, “Yoshev batel v’lo uvar aveira.” 

The daily feed gives me the best info and knowledge to become stronger every day!

The redesigned statistics page is also outstanding. Even after a fall, seeing a 78% success rate is powerful. It reminds me that I’m not a loser — I’m still doing very well.

The planning page is another major improvement. I’ve heard so much about its importance and impact, but I never fully engaged with it before. Now it’s so clear and simple — no more excuses. I fully intend to start working with it seriously.

And the updated chat system is excellent. I’ve already made so many friends here, Just yesterday I had a very difficult moment and could have fallen at any second. I quickly checked who was online, found a good fellow, and we chatted for 10–15 minutes. BH, I’m still clean.

If not for GYE, who knows where I would be today.

Thank you again for giving me hope and helping steer my life toward greatness.

With deep appreciation,

HolyAri

P.S. I also shared a thank-you post on my thread Click to view.

headline: Vaad Program is Becoming More Official
notes:
We have a new Vaad page in the Toolbox:
https://app.guardyoureyes.com/toolbox/tools/vaad, and a new group starting on Sundays at 8:30 PM EST by @Hopefulposek. Chaim/Efraim will not be doing the screening so there is less bottlenecks and more follow up.

Source: https://guardyoureyes.com/forum/profile/my-profile/userid-41036
rich_text:
Excited to share another big update on the Vaad. 


As you may have noticed, the latest site update added the Vaad as one of the official GYE programs. We made it to the big leagues, boys!


The Vaad is transitioning from a volunteer grassroots organization to something a little more functional. GYE is stepping up to take over the hard work going on behind the scenes. As the current chapter closes, and the new one opens, I think it's appropriate to thank those who got this party started.


PY and Muttel got the ball rolling in a big way, helping with the initial planning, organizing, and set up. PY stepped up to lead the first ever vaad group, definitely an accomplishment that will get him into the GYE Museum, and has been leading the project ever since.

:pinch: 


[I also helped with some of those things, but it's socially awkward to thank myself. But if I don't thank myself, someone will no doubt make some stupid comment about how I also helped. Hence, this socially awkward footnote.]


GYE staff were behind the project the whole time, with Mendy and Chaim lending considerable support and advice throughout the process. Mendy supplied the tools and worksheets for the vaad. 


Heeling and HopefulPosek stepped up to lead the other two groups. Not an easy job, and certainly one that doesn't help pay the bills. But an important job nonetheless. 


HopefulPosek has additionally stepped up and moved the project along in a big way. He's the one who gave us all a kick in the pants, pushed for, and managed the move of this project into the next iteration. He also got the group speaker series started (something we hope will continue on as a valuable part of the vaad program.) 


Other people also contributed, and continue to contribute, in important ways. I'd be remiss if I didn't mention the most faithful members of the Monday Vaad: Jolly, HeilegeEyes, and Let Me Be, whose commitment, growth and appreciation gave us the chizuk to keep the project alive. 


Keep on trucking,


Shem

headline: New Mentor Feature: Templates 🎯
rich_text:
Mentors, we just added a powerful time-saver to Live Chat: Templates.

You can now save the text and audio messages you use most often—and send them with one click instead of typing or re-recording them again and again.

Perfect for:

  • Frequently shared guidance or encouragement
  • Common explanations or next steps
  • Voice notes you find yourself repeating

Spend less time redoing the same messages, and more time being present with users.

You’ll find Templates inside Live Chat by clicking the "attachment" icon -- give it a try!

headline: Introducing Virtual Chavrusas: AI-Powered Support for GYE Tools
rich_text:
AI technology has created new challenges - but we're determined to use it for good.


Anyone who's learned with a chavrusa knows: processing complicated ideas becomes easier when you can discuss it, go back and forth, and arrive at a solution together. The same is true for recovery. Many GYE tools involve planning, brainstorming, reflecting on your situation, and strategizing about which approaches would work best for you - and this is where virtual chavrusas can really help.


This quarter, we've been building virtual chavrusas - AI-powered bots that guide you through GYE tools step by step, through natural conversation. Take our 3 Circles tool: instead of filling it out on your own, the virtual chavrusa discusses each point with you, helps you brainstorm, gives instant feedback, and then updates your actual plan on the website. It knows your context from your registration info, forum posts, and success tracker, so the conversation is personalized and productive.


We're launching with a Planning Chavrusa and a Motivation Chavrusa, rigorously tested for safety and available 24/6. These bots will live inside the same live chat platform where members can talk to mentors, volunteers, and other supportive GYE members. 


The virtual chavrusas aren't meant to replace connecting with real people - they're a supplement, available anytime to help you do the work. 


In the future, we plan to add more AI-powered bots - for example, a bot to help you stay calm during an urge, or one to help you learn lessons from a recent setback so you can prevent it from happening again - and many others. Eventually, we also plan to add voice support for a truly natural conversation. With these new technologies GYE's powerful tools are becoming more accessible, easy and engaging than ever before.


Launch date: January 15, 2026.

headline: Kedusha Challenge: A 30-Day Program for Healthier Technology Use
rich_text:
About six months ago, Yaakov Nadel showed Rabbi Eliyahu Goldwicht our 50 day Esa-Einai program for Bochurim, which we run during Shovavim. Rav Goldwicht, who heads the worldwide Smichas Chaver program with hundreds of Shuls, was greatly inspired by the concept and decided to pioneer an innovative idea: a 30-day challenge run by community rabbis for their communities, to help their members develop healthier relationships with their smartphones.


The idea was simple but powerful - participants receive one small, practical daily challenge via WhatsApp (with separate groups for men and women). Raffles at the end of the 30 days added extra motivation.


The initial rollout was a tremendous success - so far, 30 communities have participated! Each daily challenge is designed to help people become more thoughtful and less impulsive with their phone use.


GYE has now taken on the mission of scaling this initiative in a big way. We're building kedushachallenge.com as a central hub where any rabbi can access all the materials needed to bring this program to their community.


We're also creating a special teen version of the Kedusha Challenge for teenagers with smartphones, as part of our annual Esa Einai program for yeshivos - now entering its third year!


Watch the video here

headline: Finding GYE Just Got 79% Faster! We now have GYE.org!
rich_text:
Let's be honest: even our own staff admit that typing "guardyoureyes.com" requires a moment of concentration each time. Three words without spaces - it's easy to miss a letter and not even notice.


After lengthy negotiations, we've finally acquired GYE.org - and we couldn't be more excited. From 14 letters down to 3. Easy to type, easy to remember, and no more second-guessing. This simple, memorable address will soon become the official home for Guard Your Eyes and all staff emails. Finding us just got a whole lot simpler.

headline: GYE State of the Company: Q3 Wrap-Up & Q4 Rocks Update
rich_text:
Dear GYE Team,

We’ve just wrapped up Q3 of 2025 — and baruch Hashem, the momentum is real! As you might know, we’ve added our beautiful new live chat system, launched five new mini-courses, and ran a successful online campaign.

Thank you to everyone who helped make this happen!  


We invite you to watch our State of the Company video, where you’ll get a quick tour of our Q3 highlights and an inside look at what’s coming next in Q4:

  • Leveraging AI to deliver true 24/6 Chat Support

Watch the full update here: https://gye.wistia.com/medias/jm1ejs63m0

Thank you for being part of the incredible GYE team and for making this growth possible!

Yechezkel & Mendy

headline: Really Nice Testimonial
rich_text:
Dear GYE,


I’m sitting down to write a long overdue letter to those that are standing behind the scenes of the most amazing thing happening in klal Yisrael.


I’ve been part of GYE for 18 months, there have been many ups and downs contrary to when I wasn't on GYE, that there were downs and downs.


In truth, one of the reasons I didn’t yet write this letter is because I don’t know where to start and if I do manage to start, I’ll never be able to finish.


I’m just going to write some of the thoughts going through my head, you can check out my thread for more. https://gye.nu/forum/4-On-the-Way-to-90-Days/438814-Goodbye-Letter-To-PM#438814


I’m light years away from where I started, since change happens slowly, it’s hard to recognize while the progress is being made, it’s only when looking back that I can see that I’m a changed man, or more accurately, I became a live man, I was brought back to life. Had I known how hard it is to come to where I am now, I would’ve never done it, but had I tasted even one second of the freedom I have now, I would’ve done it years ago, it’s worth it even for much harder work.


Part of me didn’t want to believe I had a problem and I could overcome it myself, another part of me was deeply ashamed and didn’t want to confront it, let alone speak to someone. Now I know that I’m not weak, then I was weak and frail. I got myself a mentor (@yiftach), he’s straight up my alley, I would’ve never dreamed that a mentor could be so good.


If there is something I wish I could tell my younger self (and I’m sure there are many like my younger self out there), it would be, go join GYE, get yourself a mentor, it will show your strength and make you stronger, you’ll get back the self-esteem you badly need, you’ll stop feeling like a piece of garbage, your head will clear up, your shalom bayis is going to become real, you’ll begin tasting the taste of real intimacy and there’s nothing like it, you’ll feel true love to your kids without any guilt, in short you will come to life, every minute of being clean is worth it. There is nothing to be afraid of, he also struggled, he’s seen people of all walks of life, all ages, all backgrounds, that struggle. He has heard from them a very wide range of struggles; he’ll look up to you. There is one more thing I would like to tell my younger self, yes I’m still a critic and I didn’t believe it till I saw it and I know you won’t believe it, but the truth is that you’ll see an unexpected amount of brachach pour into your life – so go for it! Prove me wrong!


In this weeks' parsha (Bo) Rashi writes that there were jews that didn’t want to go out of Mitzrayim, on the other hand he says הללו עובדי ע''ז והללו עובדי ע''ז on the yiden that left, the difference is simple, the question is where are headed, the two groups of Jews were worlds apart, here on GYE we’re the Jews that are leaving, there still be obstacles at the yam suf etc., but we can say, הללו עבדי ה' ולא עבדי פרעה.


I would also like to tell you about the recording @HHM made, it’s a real eye opener, everyone should listen to it, it almost felt like he was talking to me. I listened to it a few times and I just couldn’t get over it, it’s a crash course that brings out the most important areas of life in a few minutes, it’s just plain brilliant! (I'm still going to listen another few times) It's extremely helpful in understanding how and why to get clean.


I know an email is to mundane to thank for a life, but at least I should say thank you,

Thank you for giving me another chance to live.


-goldwings

headline: State of Company: 2026 Q1 & Recap of Q4
GYE on CBN
rich_text:
*I can’t even begin to tell you how many ridiculous comments we got about the last post*


Like, “now kids know they can go to the library…”


And, “you blur non-tznius things but you post a video about this…”


*Seriously, you think your kids don’t know?! You think they don’t know where to get open WiFi?! Do you have any idea what has been going on in Fallsburg library during the summer?! We do that’s for sure! We need to educate, not bury!!!*


At CBN, we post the news, especially news that’s being carried on major networks. We don’t bury stories or our heads in the sand. We do what we do, even if it means going out of our comfort zone.


*We talk about things people want to bury and we have a very difficult job to do. We try our best, and we try to do what’s right for the Klal.*


This is a monster chillul Hashem, and it’s also a big challenge for all human beings. It’s a challenge that exists and always will, and we need to do better on a public level.


*I’m not concerned about this Yid. My concern lies with the children in the library and with the public bashing we are getting online.*


We encourage anyone who has similar challenges to visit the site below:


#GYE.org


*Some will disagree with me and some won’t, and that’s fine, but let’s not bury our challenges.*


Link to *CBN Unfiltered*

https://chat.whatsapp.com/HJ9nkBDhiqjKVzsaOPswVb
